calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories, And (2) For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-18-564825 Ross H. Moore VS. Unicorn Club, LTD. et al (Lead Case)
consolidated with CGC-18-564826 Ross H. Moore VS. Great Works, Inc., et al

Appearances:
Charles J. Wisch, esq. (415-788-1945) for Plaintiff.
Jamie C. Couche, esq. (415-956-6413) of Anderson & Poole for Defendant.
Court reporter: Dana Di Basilio Tognini, CSR #10118 (Tel: 415-398-1889 email: depos@nogara.com )

RULING - A R G U E D.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSS MOORE's Motion (1) To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories, And (2) For Sanctions

Granted in part and Denied in part. Request for sanctions Denied. Parties to meet and confer on language of the order. Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Dana Di Basilio Tognini, CSR #10118 (Tel: 415-398-1889 email: depos@nogara.com ) =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GREAT WORKS, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Great Works, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is GRANTED. The interest of justice is served and leave is properly granted under CCP section 428.50(c) where a proposed cross-complaint is based on facts put in issue by the complaint and answer. (Danzinger v. Peebler (1948) 88 Cal.App.2d 307, 310.) Plaintiff Ross Moore has not demonstrated any prejudice in granting leave to Defendant to file the cross-complaint. This motion was made only eight months after the complaint was filed, six months after answer filed, and only weeks after obtaining the documents and testimony needed by Defendant to support its claims. Moreover, the trial date is still several months away. Glogau v. Hagan (1951) 107 Cal.App.2d 313 does not stand for the proposition that the court should not grant leave to file any cross-complaint that might be subject to a demurrer. Rather, the case looked to various factors, including an 11 month delay from time of answer to complaint, the existence of a prior action based upon causes alleged in the cross-complaint that was still pending, as well as the fact that the claims were demurrable. The merits of the claims in the cross-complaint cannot be litigated until the cross-complaint is filed and the parties have satisfied their pre-demurrer meet and confer obligations under CCP section 430.41.

An order is signed for consolidated cases. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Newton Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/NL)
